evals.name: pasted-session-json
evals.prompt: here's a session i recorded with the chrome extension: {"sessionId":"sess-001","vendor":"cuit","createdAt":1748952000000,"url":"http://localhost:5173","events":[{"seq":0,"vendor":"cuit","vendorEventId":"e0","ts":0,"wallClock":1748952000000,"type":"nav","url":"http://localhost:5173"},{"seq":1,"vendor":"cuit","vendorEventId":"e1","ts":100,"wallClock":1748952000100,"type":"pointer","phase":"down","targetName":"seg-0","x":40,"y":32,"pointerId":1}]}. can you generate a spec and run it against my app?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Claude calls @cuit/spec-gen, generates a .spec.ts, runs it, auto-detects flow A vs B based on first-run outcome.

evals.name: attached-session-file
evals.prompt: i attached a cuit-session.json file. close the loop please — generate a spec from it and verify it gates the bug i was reproducing.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Reads the attached file, runs spec-gen, executes the spec, reports RED/GREEN with flow auto-detection.

evals.name: explicit-cuit-loop-mention
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Explicit invocation against a fixture path.

evals.name: vendor-adapter-session
evals.prompt: I have a Jam session export at /tmp/jam-export.json — can you run it through the cuit loop and create a regression test from what the user did?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Normalizes the Jam session via the adapter, runs spec-gen, executes.

evals.name: baseline-lock-in-flow-b
evals.prompt: Here's a session JSON of me using the brand new checkout flow on staging — everything works. Can you lock it in as a regression baseline so we catch any future drift?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Generates spec, runs against staging, expects GREEN first time, commits as Flow B baseline lock-in.

evals.name: instrument-not-loop
evals.prompt: i just signed up for CUIT and want to wire it into my Next.js app. how do i actually get it set up so my dev sessions start flowing to the warehouse?
evals.expected_output: /cuit-instrument should trigger, NOT /cuit-loop. This is wire-up, not loop-running. The two skills are explicitly partitioned by whether the app is already instrumented.

evals.name: generic-spec-question
evals.prompt: how does playwright handle drag and drop events? mine keep flaking randomly.
evals.expected_output: Neither skill should trigger. No session JSON, no CUIT context — just a generic Playwright debugging question.

evals.name: no-session-just-bug-report
evals.prompt: users keep reporting that the segment drag bug is back in production. can you investigate and write a regression test?
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger /cuit-loop without a session. Claude should ask the developer to first record the bug with the recorder (then run /cuit-loop). Or suggest /cuit-instrument if the app isn't wired up yet.

evals.name: ambiguous-spec-from-bug
evals.prompt: write me a playwright regression spec for this bug: when you drag a segment past another, the first one snaps back instead of moving.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger /cuit-loop (no session JSON). Claude should write a hand-authored Playwright spec from the prose description OR suggest using the recorder + /cuit-loop instead.

evals.name: session-without-loop-intent
evals.prompt: got this from the chrome extension. dunno what to do with it next. cuit-session.json attached.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger /cuit-loop. The user has a session JSON and is uncertain what to do — /cuit-loop is exactly the right routing.
